_t16_power_level_note: PLACEHOLDER pending a real server-side tier signal (see palette_math.gd doc-comment). default_power_level applies to every agent; class_power_levels is an OPTIONAL demo scaffold that spreads the 5 named bands across classes so a live tower visibly demonstrates the palette-swap shader until SSEAgentRegistered carries a real tier field.
_t16_providers_note: Color stops for the palette-swap shader's per-provider LUT (procedural GradientTexture1D, see provider_palette.gd). Seeded IDENTICAL to floating_rune.gd PROVIDER_COLORS as a shared reference; floating_rune.gd is NOT refactored to read this config this task (avoids regressing rune tinting) — TODO future unification.
_t17_particles_note: T17 (#127) tier particle effects. max_particles_per_agent is the sole budget-cap knob (acceptance #5), threaded once-per-floor (global, not per-agent) via FloorScene.configure_particle_budget(). Each provider block's optional particle_style selects a procedural shape family (dot/spark/shard/ring — see particle_textures.gd); 'rune'/'leaf' (Claude/OpenAI) intentionally fall back to 'dot' today — bespoke glyph/leaf art is deferred to T22. Particle COLOR reuses the same stops above via ProviderPalette.get_accent_color() — no second color table. New provider = one block here, zero code (acceptance #4).
